1	package bead
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"io"
6	)
7	
8	// RawBackend is the low-level storage contract — read/write the entire bead
9	// corpus and serialize concurrent rewrites. JSONLBackend and ExternalBackend
10	// implement it. New backends should implement Backend directly; RawBackend is
11	// retained for the JSONL/external composition path that Store uses internally.
12	// Higher-level operations (CRUD, claim, ready/blocked, dep ops, events,
13	// archive, JSONL interchange) live on the Backend interface below and are
14	// composed on top of a RawBackend by Store.
15	type RawBackend interface {
16		Init() error
17		ReadAll() ([]Bead, error)
18		WriteAll(beads []Bead) error
19		WithLock(fn func() error) error
20	}
21	
22	// Backend is the high-level bead-tracker contract that callers in
23	// cli/cmd/bead_*.go and the agent loop will eventually program against. It
24	// covers everything the bead description for ddx-bbdd7564 calls out: CRUD,
25	// claim, list/ready/blocked, dep ops, events append, archive split, and
26	// JSONL export/import.
27	//
28	// *Store satisfies this interface; the JSONL implementation lives in store.go
29	// and delegates per-write serialization to the configured RawBackend
30	// (JSONLBackend by default, ExternalBackend when DDX_BEAD_BACKEND or the
31	// .ddx/config.yaml beads.backend field selects bd/br).
32	//
33	// chaos_test.go and other conformance suites should program against this
34	// interface so additional backends can be exercised by the same tests.
35	type Backend interface {
36		// Foundational
37		Init(args ...any) error
38		ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
39	
40		// CRUD
41		Create(args ...any) error
42		Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)
43		Update(args ...any) error
44		Close(args ...any) error
45	
46		// Claim
47		Claim(id, assignee string) error
48		ClaimWithOptions(id, assignee, session, worktree string) error
49		Unclaim(id string) error
50	
51		// Query
52		List(status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
53		Ready() ([]Bead, error)
54		Blocked() ([]Bead, error)
55	
56		// Dep ops
57		DepAdd(id, depID string) error
58		DepRemove(id, depID string) error
59		DepTree(rootID string) (string, error)
60	
61		// Events
62		AppendEvent(id string, event BeadEvent) error
63		Events(id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
64	
65		// Archive split
66		Archive(policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
67		Migrate() (MigrateStats, error)
68	
69		// JSONL interchange
70		Import(source, filePath string) (int, error)
71		ExportTo(w io.Writer) error
72	}
73	
74	// TD-027 foundation interfaces. These are additive and intentionally do not
75	// change the legacy Store-backed interface above in this bead slice.
76	type BeadInitializer interface {
77		Init(args ...any) error
78	}
79	
80	type BeadReader interface {
81		ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
82		ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)
83		Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)
84	}
85	
86	type BeadLifecycle interface {
87		Create(args ...any) error
88		Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error
89	}
90	
91	type BeadEventReader interface {
92		Events(ctx context.Context, id string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
93		EventsByKind(ctx context.Context, id, kind string) ([]BeadEvent, error)
94	}
95	
96	type BeadEventWriter interface {
97		AppendEvent(ctx context.Context, id string, event BeadEvent) error
98	}
99	
100	type BeadQueries interface {
101		List(ctx context.Context, status, label string, where map[string]string) ([]Bead, error)
102		Ready(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
103		Blocked(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
104		ReadyExecutionBreakdown(ctx context.Context) (ReadyExecutionBreakdown, error)
105		ProposedOperatorAttention(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
106		NeedsHuman(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
107		ExternalBlocked(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
108		DependencyWaiting(ctx context.Context) ([]Bead, error)
109		BlockedAll(ctx context.Context) ([]BlockedBead, error)
110		Status(ctx context.Context) (*StatusCounts, error)
111	}
112	
113	type BeadDependencyReader interface {
114		DepTree(ctx context.Context, rootID string) (string, error)
115	}
116	
117	type BeadDependencyWriter interface {
118		DepAdd(ctx context.Context, id, depID string) error
119		DepRemove(ctx context.Context, id, depID string) error
120	}
121	
122	type BeadArchive interface {
123		Archive(ctx context.Context, policy ArchivePolicy) ([]string, error)
124		Migrate(ctx context.Context) (MigrateStats, error)
125	}
126	
127	type BeadInterchangeReader interface {
128		ExportTo(ctx context.Context, w io.Writer) error
129	}
130	
131	type BeadInterchangeWriter interface {
132		Import(ctx context.Context, source, filePath string) (int, error)
133	}
134	
135	type ReadOnlyBackend interface {
136		BeadInitializer
137		BeadReader
138		BeadEventReader
139		BeadQueries
140		BeadDependencyReader
141		BeadInterchangeReader
142	}
143	
144	// LifecycleSubscriber is the parallel subscription surface from TD-027.
145	type LifecycleSubscriber interface {
146		SubscribeLifecycle(ctx context.Context, projectID string) (<-chan LifecycleEvent, func(), error)
147	}
148	
149	// OperationApplier is an optional fast path for RawBackend implementations
150	// that can preserve specialized mutation behavior.
151	type OperationApplier interface {
152		Apply(ctx context.Context, id string, op Operation) error
153	}
154	
155	// BackendType constants
156	const (
157		BackendJSONL = "jsonl"
158		BackendBD    = "bd"
159		BackendBR    = "br"
160	)
161	
162	const DefaultCollection = "beads"

565	      tags: [policy]
566	
567	    # AGENTS.md §Merge Policy: branches carrying execute-bead / [ddx-*]
568	    # commits must preserve SHA history. closing_commit_sha pointers in
569	    # .ddx/beads.jsonl index those exact commits; squash/rebase/amend that
570	    # rewrites them destroys the output = bead(input) guarantee.
571	    # This guard rejects a push if the local branch contains execute-bead
572	    # or [ddx-*] tagged commits AND the remote already has a different
573	    # history for them (i.e. a force-push rewrite is in flight).
574	    merge-policy:
575	      name: "Preserve execute-bead history"
576	      run: |
577	        # {1} = remote name (origin); {2} = remote URL
578	        remote="${1:-origin}"
579	        # Gather the commits being pushed. lefthook sets {push_files}
580	        # for file pushes; for commit-level inspection we use git range.
581	        # Find the remote-tracking branch HEAD to compute what's new.
582	        local_ref="$(git symbolic-ref -q HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
583	        [ -z "$local_ref" ] && exit 0
584	        remote_ref="refs/remotes/$remote/${local_ref#refs/heads/}"
585	        if ! git rev-parse --verify "$remote_ref" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
586	          # No remote tracking yet — first push, nothing to rewrite.
587	          exit 0
588	        fi
589	        # Commits local has that remote does not (being pushed).
590	        new_commits="$(git log --format='%H %s' "$remote_ref"..HEAD 2>/dev/null)"
591	        [ -z "$new_commits" ] && exit 0
592	        # Commits remote has that local dropped (rewritten or pruned).
593	        dropped="$(git log --format='%H %s' HEAD.."$remote_ref" 2>/dev/null)"
594	        [ -z "$dropped" ] && exit 0
595	        # Check for execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers in dropped commits.
596	        pattern='execute-bead|\[ddx-[0-9a-f]{8,}\]'
597	        if printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -qE "$pattern"; then
598	          echo "ERROR: push would drop commits with execute-bead / [ddx-*] markers:"
599	          printf '%s\n' "$dropped" | grep -E "$pattern" | head
600	          echo ""
601	          echo "AGENTS.md §Merge Policy forbids rewriting these SHAs because"
602	          echo ".ddx/beads.jsonl closing_commit_sha points at them."
603	          echo "Use --ff-only or --no-ff; do not squash, rebase, or amend."
604	          exit 1
605	        fi
606	        exit 0
607	      tags: [policy]
608	
609	    # Ensure no debug statements in any file
610	    no-debug:
611	      name: "No debug statements"
612	      run: |
613	        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
614	          rg "(console\.log|console\.debug\(|console\.error\(\"DEBUG|fmt\.Print(ln|f)?\(\"DEBUG|pdb\.set_trace|debugger|breakpoint\(\))" \
615	            --type-add 'code:*.{go,js,jsx,ts,tsx,py}' -t code
616	          status=$?
617	          # rg exit codes: 0=match found (fail), 1=no match (pass), 2+=error (fail closed)
618	          if [ "$status" -eq 1 ]; then
619	            exit 0
620	          fi

622	        fi

status: proposed
reason: bead AC1+AC3 contradict the explicit non-scope; needs operator re-scoping

Findings (worktree base-rev 86de1c4af, .ddx/executions/20260516T043007-7ac09885):

1. Current state of the foundational Store methods (cli/internal/bead/store.go):
     Init(args ...any) error                                  // line 149
     GenID(args ...any) (string, error)                       // line 167
     ReadAll(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)                     // line 182
     ReadAllFiltered(args ...any) ([]Bead, error)             // line 224
     Create(args ...any) error                                // line 518
     Get(args ...any) (*Bead, error)                          // line 598
     Update(args ...any) error                                // line 627
     Close(args ...any) error                                 // line 1426
   And in archive.go:
     GetWithArchive(id string) (*Bead, error)                 // line 172
   Each variadic method extracts ctx via storeCallContext(args) and accepts
   either `s.Init()` or `s.Init(context.Background())` today.

2. AC1 requires these methods to take `ctx context.Context` as a typed first
   parameter (matching the BeadEventReader / BeadQueries shape already in
   cli/internal/bead/backend.go:91-114). Any concrete signature satisfying
   AC1 — `Init(ctx context.Context) error`, `Init(ctx context.Context, args ...any) error`,
   etc. — rejects the current zero-arg `s.Init()` callsites that exist in
   external packages.

3. The bead's PROPOSED FIX and NON-SCOPE explicitly forbid touching external
   callers ("Do not touch the external caller sweep in this bead";
   "NON-SCOPE: Caller migration in cli/cmd, cli/internal/agent,
   cli/internal/server, cli/internal/escalation, and metrics packages.")
   and AC4 requires "no caller-package changes in this bead."

4. AC3 requires `lefthook run pre-commit` to pass, which runs
   `go build -buildvcs=false -v ./...` from cli/ (lefthook.yml:122-129).
   That build covers every package in the module, including all the
   external caller packages NON-SCOPE forbids touching.

5. Concrete external breakage if AC1 is implemented today
   (`grep -rE '\b(store|s)\.(Init|Create|Update|Get|Close|ReadAll|GenID|GetWithArchive|ReadAllFiltered)\(' cli/ --include='*.go'`):
     - 174 Go files reference these Store methods.
     - 73 distinct files outside cli/internal/bead/ use the zero-arg
       `store.Init()` form (e.g. cli/cmd/ac_test.go:37,
       cli/cmd/bead_human_test.go:20, cli/internal/agent/...,
       cli/internal/server/...).
     - Production-code stragglers without ctx exist too:
         cli/cmd/bead.go:414  s.GetWithArchive(args[0])
         cli/cmd/bead.go:1281 s.Close(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4485 store.Get(id)
         cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:4811 store.Update(beadID, ...)
   `go build ./...` and the lefthook go-build hook will fail at every one
   of those callsites the instant Init/Get/Update/Close/ReadAll/GenID/
   GetWithArchive/ReadAllFiltered become non-variadic ctx-first. There is
   no signature shape that satisfies AC1 while preserving all current
   call patterns (Go does not allow same-name method overloads on a
   single receiver type).

6. Step 0 size envelope is also exceeded for the only resolution that
   preserves AC1+AC3: the necessary mechanical caller sweep crosses ~170
   files in five packages (cmd, internal/agent, internal/server,
   internal/attemptmetrics, internal/agentmetrics) — well past the
   "~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages" guardrail. That
   is exactly the sweep that NON-SCOPE excludes, and the parent split
   (TD-027 / ddx-464feba2) was rejected for the same reason.

Operator action requested:

  Re-scope this bead to one of:

  (a) Add the caller-package migration to scope (delete the NON-SCOPE
      block and AC4's "no caller-package changes"). This becomes the
      original TD-027 sweep that previously failed as too large; if
      pursued, expect a multi-file diff across cli/cmd,
      cli/internal/agent, cli/internal/server,
      cli/internal/attemptmetrics, cli/internal/agentmetrics.

  (b) Replace AC3 with a narrower verification (e.g. "go test
      ./internal/bead/... and go vet ./internal/bead/... pass"), and
      explicitly accept that `go build ./...` and lefthook pre-commit
      will be red until follow-up caller-migration beads land. The
      follow-up beads would then re-establish AC3 once the sweep is
      complete.

  (c) Replace AC1 with an additive shape that does not rename or
      retype the existing variadic methods — e.g. "add new ctx-first
      Store methods with distinct names (InitCtx / GetCtx / …) so the
      legacy variadic surface stays in place during the migration",
      then a follow-up bead deletes the variadic methods after the
      caller sweep finishes. This satisfies the spirit of TD-027
      (introducing the typed ctx-first surface in the bead package
      first) without breaking the build outside the package.

No code changes were made on this attempt. Request was triaged
strictly against the bead body — no /tmp scratch files, no work
outside the worktree.
